description: v0.4 M1b — linux-x64 baseline. Carried over unchanged from v0.3e-linux-x64.json; the native code path didn't regress when we added the win-x64 job, so the same primitives apply. (macOS/ARM support was dropped from v0.4 scope — see perf-gate.yml matrix.) Repin via `python3 scripts/pfsf_perf_gate.py --update-baseline --results ... --baseline benchmarks/baselines/v0.4-linux-x64.json` on the ubuntu-22.04 runner after deliberate perf changes.

runner.note: GitHub Actions ubuntu-22.04 runner fleet (2-core @ ~3.25 GHz, 7 GB RAM). -march=x86-64-v3 is load-bearing for the native speedup targets.

primitives.name: normalize_soa6_64k
primitives.note: Bandwidth-bound 64k × 7 floats. Java 17 HotSpot JIT generates competitive vectorised code, native_over_java ~1.09×. Native floor disabled pending M3 target_clones FMV.

primitives.name: chebyshev_table_64
primitives.note: JNI boundary + JIT warmup dominate at this size. Native check intentionally off.

primitives.name: apply_wind_bias_64k
primitives.note: Six direction passes, scalar multiply. Native needs -march=x86-64-v3 to hit 1.5× floor.

primitives.name: tick50k_surrogate
primitives.note: Aggregate surrogate: 100 reps × (normalize + wind bias + omega). Native floor disabled until M3 plan-buffer JNI amortisation.
